name: Tadalafil Exercise Capacity And Physical Function Baseline Review
summary: Stages the first baseline for tadalafil exercise and physical function: a targeted search protocol, direct tadalafil healthy-performance and disease-function sources, structured artifacts/findings, adjacent sildenafil hypoxia evidence, and one bounded baseline claim.

scope.research_question: What is the current evidence landscape for tadalafil and PDE5 inhibitors on exercise capacity, physical performance, fatigue, recovery, and physical function, especially for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50?

proposed_changes.summary: Add targeted exercise and physical-function search protocol.
proposed_changes.rationale: The baseline needs transparent screening boundaries for healthy performance, disease-specific functional outcomes, and adjacent PDE5 evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add source The long-acting phosphodiesterase inhibitor tadalafil does not influence athletes' VO2max, aerobic, and anaerobic thresholds in normoxia.
proposed_changes.rationale: The source is included in the first exercise and physical-function baseline evidence map.

proposed_changes.summary: Add source Effect of tadalafil on anaerobic performance indices in healthy athletes.
proposed_changes.rationale: The source is included in the first exercise and physical-function baseline evidence map.

proposed_changes.summary: Add source The phosphodiesterases type 5 inhibitor tadalafil reduces the activation of the hypothalamus-pituitary-adrenal axis in men during cycle ergometric exercise.
proposed_changes.rationale: The source is included in the first exercise and physical-function baseline evidence map.

proposed_changes.summary: Add source Effects of tadalafil administration on plasma markers of exercise-induced muscle damage, IL6 and antioxidant status capacity.
proposed_changes.rationale: The source is included in the first exercise and physical-function baseline evidence map.

proposed_changes.rationale: The source is included in the first exercise and physical-function baseline evidence map.

proposed_changes.summary: Add source The effect of tadalafil on the time to exercise-induced myocardial ischaemia in subjects with coronary artery disease.
proposed_changes.rationale: The source is included in the first exercise and physical-function baseline evidence map.

proposed_changes.summary: Add source Sildenafil does not reliably improve exercise performance in hypoxia: a systematic review.
proposed_changes.rationale: The source is included in the first exercise and physical-function baseline evidence map.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Acute tadalafil did not improve VO2max or exercise thresholds in healthy male athletes
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Acute tadalafil did not substantially improve Wingate anaerobic performance
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Tadalafil changed exercise stress-axis hormones without improving performance parameters
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Short tadalafil exposure worsened oxidative and muscle-damage markers after exhaustive exercise
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Acute tadalafil increased muscle-damage markers without antioxidant benefit in fit men
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Daily tadalafil increased abdominal lean mass in mild ED/LUTS but did not test strength or exercise performance
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Tadalafil restored exercising muscle blood-flow regulation in Becker muscular dystrophy
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Phase 3 DMD trial found tadalafil did not slow ambulatory decline
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Tadalafil did not improve 6-minute walk distance in COPD-associated pulmonary hypertension
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Tadalafil did not change exercise-induced ischemia time in CAD but lowered blood pressure and reinforced nitrate risk
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add finding Sildenafil hypoxia review supports only adjacent and unreliable PDE5 exercise-performance transfer
proposed_changes.rationale: The finding supports or bounds the exercise and physical-function baseline claim.

proposed_changes.summary: Add bounded baseline claim for tadalafil exercise and physical function.
proposed_changes.rationale: The first public baseline should state the insufficiency of healthy-user ergogenic evidence while preserving disease-specific signals and safety boundaries.

proposed_claim_implications.note: Creates the first submitted baseline claim for tadalafil exercise capacity and physical function.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
endpoint_validity: No correction required; preserve endpoint-boundary wording in the claim and public copy.
exposure_directness: No correction required; retrieve full text before dose-specific synthesis and keep sildenafil as adjacent non-tadalafil evidence.
population_directness: No correction required; preserve disease-specific and symptom-selected population qualifiers in public summaries.
safety_risk_framing: No correction required; keep the dedicated safety baseline attached and avoid comparative AE synthesis without full trial reports.
source_fidelity: No correction required for baseline publication; retrieve full text before quantitative performance, recovery, or muscular-dystrophy synthesis.
synthesis_overreach: No correction required; preserve the negative-to-insufficient healthy-user conclusion in public summaries.
